name: Alussa Energy Acquisition Corp. II
business: Alussa Energy Acquisition Corp. II is a blank check company formed to effect a merger or business combination with one or more businesses, with a focus on the energy and power infrastructure sectors, particularly those benefiting from the transition to renewable energy. It has no operating revenues to date and has not yet identified a target. The company raised $287.5 million in its IPO to fund such a combination.

exposures.sentence: We intend to focus on businesses in the energy and power infrastructure sectors; particularly those that are empowering or beneficiaries of the continued transition towards renewable energy.

We believe the energy transition is still in its infancy and that the mentioned trends in clean energy supply and demand sub-sectors may continue to create downstream opportunities across a host of subsectors, including power generation, energy efficiency and demand side flexibility solutions, battery storage, nuclear power, grid technologies and infrastructure, material recovery and reuse, and others.

We believe the energy transition is still in its infancy and that the mentioned trends in clean energy supply and demand sub-sectors may continue to create downstream opportunities across a host of subsectors, including power generation, energy efficiency and demand side flexibility solutions, battery storage, nuclear power, grid technologies and infrastructure, material recovery and reuse, and others.

We believe the energy transition is still in its infancy and that the mentioned trends in clean energy supply and demand sub-sectors may continue to create downstream opportunities across a host of subsectors, including power generation, energy efficiency and demand side flexibility solutions, battery storage, nuclear power, grid technologies and infrastructure, material recovery and reuse, and others.

We will also look for opportunities that employ traditional energy sources to bridge the long-term transition to more renewable and sustainable sources which could range from power plants to liquified natural gas ( LNG ) adjacent opportunities.

Increasing reliance on data center infrastructure and computing power and global population pressures have exacerbated grid instability, congestion, and lack thereof predominantly in locations without access to large-scale, traditional frequency response technologies.

We also anticipate impacts to ancillary sub-sectors at the cross-section of energy and technology such as data centers, electric vehicles, virtual power plants, carbon capture, intelligent grid technologies, and others.

catalysts.event: Deadline to complete initial business combination
catalysts.sentence: We must complete our initial Business Combination by November 14, 2027, which is 24 months from the closing of our Initial Public Offering, unless we seek and obtain shareholder approval to amend our Amended and Restated Charter in order to extend the Combination Period.

tamClaims.claim: Global investment in clean and traditional energy is set to exceed a combined $3 trillion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the International Energy Agency ( IEA ), global investment in clean and traditional energy is set to exceed a combined $3 trillion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Total $1.77 trillion invested across all clean energy sub-sectors in 2023.
tamClaims.sentence: Worldwide recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic and energy crisis thereafter provided significant support for global clean energy investments, with a total $1.77 trillion invested across all clean energy sub-sectors in 2023 relative to $1.09 trillion in traditional energy investment.
